items.final_wording: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Keloids and Hypertrophic Scars—Japan Scar Workshop Consensus Document 2018
source.jurisdiction: Japan; international scar-consensus evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People with a possible or established keloid or hypertrophic scar; the document highlights greater susceptibility in Asian and African populations.
source.setting: Primary care, dermatology, plastic-surgery, and specialist scar assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting scar origin and course, location, symptoms, clinical appearance, growth relative to the original wound, and clinician-assessed scar severity. It does not authorise automatic diagnosis, scoring, biopsy, treatment, procedure, referral, or follow-up.

exact_location.heading: Diagnostic algorithm for pathological scars and clinical diagnosis of keloids and hypertrophic scars
exact_location.locator: publisher full text, Main context, lines 132–186

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

items.rationale: The exact scar diagnostic section supports documenting a possible or established keloid, including onset, progression, location and growth beyond the original wound boundary, while explicitly preserving uncertainty between keloid, hypertrophic scar, mature scar, and mimics.

items.final_wording: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Keloids and Hypertrophic Scars—Japan Scar Workshop Consensus Document 2018
source.jurisdiction: Japan; international scar-consensus evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People with a possible or established keloid or hypertrophic scar; the document highlights greater susceptibility in Asian and African populations.
source.setting: Primary care, dermatology, plastic-surgery, and specialist scar assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting scar origin and course, location, symptoms, clinical appearance, growth relative to the original wound, and clinician-assessed scar severity. It does not authorise automatic diagnosis, scoring, biopsy, treatment, procedure, referral, or follow-up.

exact_location.heading: Treatment algorithms at different medical facilities — assessment before treatment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.

items.rationale: The specialist assessment points support documenting clinician-assessed severity, subjective symptoms, functional impact, lesion site and prior response; no JSS score, procedure, treatment, or referral is generated.
